text:
DigiCert impl complete. Created:
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/digicert/config.yaml` - 5 resources (order, certificate, organization, domain, user), 2 operations (create_order, revoke_certificate). Auth via X-DC-DEVKEY header, base URL https://www.digicert.com/services/v2.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/digicert/axioms.dl` - Declarations for all 5 resources, enumerations for order status, product type, signature hash, payment method. Derived relations for active orgs, issued/pending orders, active domains. Invariants on order status and product type.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/digicert/README.md` - Service overview.

Researched sapcc/go-certcentral for API structure: base URL, auth header, endpoint paths, struct fields, product types, and payment methods all derived from the Go client source.

Task #5 done. Created impls/secret_server/ with config.yaml, axioms.dl, README.md.

- Referenced DelineaXPM/tss-sdk-go for API surface
- Used impls/delinea/ as structural reference (same product, different branding)
- Resources: secret, folder, user, role
- Operations: create_secret, get_secret, delete_secret
- Auth: bearer token at /api/v1, same shape as delinea impl
- User relation dropped is_application_account (not in Go SDK surface), added role resource per task spec

oracle_cloud impl complete. Created 3 files:

- `impls/oracle_cloud/config.yaml` — 6 resources (compartment, instance, vcn, subnet, user, group), 12 operations (CRUD + instance_action + add_user_to_group). Auth modeled as api_key per task spec. OCI API version prefix 20160918, page_token pagination via opc-next-page header.
- `impls/oracle_cloud/axioms.dl` — 6 relation decls, lifecycle state enums (general + instance-specific), derived relations (running/stopped instances, vcn_subnet, active_user), mutation decls for all 12 ops, invariants on lifecycle_state for all resources.
- `impls/oracle_cloud/README.md` — brief summary.

Notable OCI conventions reflected: camelCase JSON field names in CEL expressions (compartmentId, displayName, lifecycleState), regional base_url, compartment_id required on all list operations.

Mode impl complete and validates clean (ok: true). Files written:

- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/mode/config.yaml` -- 5 resources (space, membership, report, query, report_run), 7 operations (create/delete space, delete membership, create report_run, archive report, create/delete query). All endpoints org-scoped, basic auth, HAL+JSON responses.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/mode/axioms.dl` -- 5 relation decls, enumerations for space_type/membership_state/run_state, 3 derived relations, mutation declarations, 3 invariants.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/mode/README.md`

Source: dwallace0723/pymode client.py (Mode Analytics v2 API). The one validation warning is a false positive -- synthetic test data generates "active" as a run state but our valid_run_state enum correctly doesn't include it (it's a membership state).

Rippling impl complete. Created all three files in `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/rippling/`:

- **config.yaml** - 8 resources (employee, company, department, group, team, work_location, level, leave_request), 6 operations (CRUD groups, leave request lifecycle). Base URL `https://api.rippling.com`, bearer auth, cursor pagination. All endpoints under `/platform/api/`.
- **axioms.dl** - Declarations for all 8 resources, valid enumerations for role_state (5 values), employment_type (6), leave_status (4), leave_type (3), process_action (2). Derived relations: active_employee, terminated_employee, manager_employee, subdepartment. Mutation and invariant declarations for all 6 operations.
- **README.md** - Summary with provenance (stellaraf/go-rippling, autogenerated from Rippling OpenAPI spec).

API shape confirmed from stellaraf/go-rippling generated client code (oapi-codegen from official Rippling OpenAPI spec at developer.rippling.com).

OpenVPN impl complete. Created:

- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/openvpn/config.yaml` — 9 resources (network, host, user, user_group, network_connector, host_connector, dns_record, vpn_region, access_group), 12 operations (CRUD for network, host, user, user_group, dns_record, access_group). OAuth2 bearer auth, page-based pagination on `$.content`.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/openvpn/axioms.dl` — 9 relation decls, derived rules (admin/active/connected), mutation decls, invariants on user role/status.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/openvpn/README.md`

Source: OpenVPN/cloudconnexa-go-client on GitHub (6 stars, official Go client). API is v1 at `https://INSTANCE.api.openvpn.com/api/v1`. All struct fields and endpoint paths verified against the Go client source.

Retool impl complete. Three files written:

- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/retool/config.yaml` -- 6 resources (user, group, folder, app, space, environment), 11 operations (CRUD for users/groups/folders/spaces, group membership, permission grant/revoke). Bearer auth, cursor pagination via next_token. Base URL is instance-specific.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/retool/axioms.dl` -- Declarations for all 6 resources, valid enumerations (folder_type, universal_access, user_type, access_level), derived relations (active/admin users, full-access groups, system folders, protected/module apps), mutation declarations for all 11 operations, 4 invariants.
- `/Users/rch/repo/mcp-axiomatic/impls/retool/README.md` -- Brief overview.

Source: tryretool/terraform-provider-retool OpenAPI spec v2.12.0 (from their generated SDK). The API has many more endpoints (source control, observability, SSO, workflows, etc.) but I focused on the core resource management surface the task specified.

Task #4 complete. Wrote `impls/formal/` with config.yaml, axioms.dl, README.md.

Resources modeled: datastore, policy, group, user, native_user_link.
Operations: CRUD for policy and group, create/delete for datastore and native_user_link.
Validation passes clean (no errors, no warnings).

Research notes:
- Formal's actual API uses Connect RPC (HTTP-based gRPC) at `https://api.joinformal.com` with `X-Api-Key` header auth (not bearer). Used task-specified base_url `https://api.formalcloud.net/v1` per instructions.
- Source: formalco/terraform-provider-formal (3 stars) and formalco/go-sdk. The terraform provider has ~38 resource types; I modeled the 5 specified in the task.
- The task mentioned "role" and "native_role_link" as resources. The provider has no "role" resource -- it has users with types (human/machine), groups, and native_user_links (linking native DB users to Formal identities). I modeled native_user_link which is the closest match.
Acknowledged. oracle_cloud impl was my only task and it's done. Ready to shut down.
Acknowledged. Formal impl was delivered and validated. Ready to shut down.
Acknowledged. Retool impl was my only task and it's complete. Ready to shut down.
